Class ExpenseTracker

java.lang.Object
controller.ExpenseTracker
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tracker<Expense>
public class ExpenseTracker extends Object implements Tracker<Expense>
ExpenseTracker implements the Tracker interface to manage user expenses. This class follows the Singleton pattern to ensure only one instance exists. It provides functionality for adding, updating, deleting, and analyzing expense data, as well as persisting expense data to the file system.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static ExpenseTracker getInstance()
      Gets the singleton instance of ExpenseTracker. Creates a new instance if none exists.
      Returns:
      The singleton instance of ExpenseTracker

    • getExpenses

      public ArrayList<Expense> getExpenses()
      Gets the list of all expenses.
      Returns:
      An ArrayList containing all expense records

    • add

      public void add(Expense expense)
      Adds a new expense to the tracker.
      Specified by:
      add in interface Tracker<Expense>
      Parameters:
      expense - The expense object to add

    • edit

      public void edit(Expense expense)
      Edits an existing expense in the tracker. Finds the expense with matching ID and replaces it.
      Specified by:
      edit in interface Tracker<Expense>
      Parameters:
      expense - The updated expense object

    • delete

      public void delete(String expenseId)
      Deletes an expense from the tracker by its ID.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface Tracker<Expense>
      Parameters:
      expenseId - The ID of the expense to delete

    • validate

      public boolean validate(Expense expense)
      Validates an expense object to ensure it contains all required fields.
      Specified by:
      validate in interface Tracker<Expense>
      Parameters:
      expense - The expense object to validate
      Returns:
      true if the expense is valid, false otherwise

    • calculateAmount

      public BigDecimal calculateAmount()
      Calculates the total amount of all expenses.
      Specified by:
      calculateAmount in interface Tracker<Expense>
      Returns:
      The total amount as a BigDecimal

    • addExpense

      public void addExpense(Expense expense)
      Legacy method to add an expense. Calls the standardized add method.
      Parameters:
      expense - The expense object to add

    • updateExpense

      public void updateExpense(Expense expense)
      Legacy method to update an expense. Calls the standardized edit method.
      Parameters:
      expense - The updated expense object

    • removeExpense

      public void removeExpense(String expenseId)
      Legacy method to remove an expense. Calls the standardized delete method.
      Parameters:
      expenseId - The ID of the expense to remove

    • getTotalExpenses

      public double getTotalExpenses()
      Gets the total of all expenses as a double value.
      Returns:
      The total amount of all expenses

    • getExpensesByDateRange

      public List<Expense> getExpensesByDateRange(LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate)
      Gets all expenses within a specified date range.
      Parameters:
      startDate - The start date of the range (inclusive)
      endDate - The end date of the range (inclusive)
      Returns:
      A list of expenses within the specified date range

    • getExpensesForMonth

      public List<Expense> getExpensesForMonth(int year, int month)
      Gets all expenses for a specific month.
      Parameters:
      year - The year
      month - The month (1-12)
      Returns:
      A list of expenses for the specified month

    • getCategoryTotals

      public Map<String,Double> getCategoryTotals()
      Calculates the total expense amount for each category.
      Returns:
      A map with category names as keys and total amounts as values

    • clearData

      public void clearData()
      Clears all expense data from memory.

    • saveData

      public boolean saveData(int userId)
      Saves expense data to a file for a specific user.
      Parameters:
      userId - The ID of the user
      Returns:
      true if data was saved successfully, false otherwise

    • loadData

      public boolean loadData(int userId)
      Loads expense data from a file for a specific user. If no file exists, initializes an empty expense list.
      Parameters:
      userId - The ID of the user
      Returns:
      true if data was loaded successfully or if a new list was created, false on error
